source: Okay, 71-year-old man, history of COPD and type 2 diabetes, brought in by EMS for altered mental status and fever. Per the family he's been more confused since yesterday, decreased oral intake, and they noticed he was breathing fast this morning. On arrival he's febrile to 39.2, heart rate 122, blood pressure 88 over 52, respiratory rate 26, satting 91 on room air. He's lethargic but arousable, mucous membranes dry. Lungs have right basilar crackles. I'm concerned about sepsis, likely pulmonary source. We drew a lactate, it's 3.8. Started him on a 30 cc per kilo crystalloid bolus, drew blood cultures before antibiotics, gave broad-spectrum — piperacillin-tazobactam. Chest x-ray shows a right lower lobe infiltrate. After the bolus his pressure came up to 102 over 64. I spent about 40 minutes at the bedside managing his resuscitation given the hemodynamic instability. Admitting to the ICU, sepsis from community-acquired pneumonia.
reference:
HPI: 71M with COPD and T2DM, brought in by EMS for altered mental status and fever, with one day of worsening confusion, decreased PO intake, and tachypnea per family.

Vitals: T 39.2, HR 122, BP 88/52, RR 26, SpO2 91% RA.
Exam: Lethargic but arousable, dry mucous membranes, right basilar crackles.
Data: Lactate 3.8. CXR: right lower lobe infiltrate.

ED course: Sepsis with pulmonary source suspected. 30 cc/kg crystalloid bolus, blood cultures drawn before antibiotics, piperacillin-tazobactam given. BP improved to 102/64 post-bolus. Critical care time: 40 minutes at the bedside managing resuscitation given hemodynamic instability.

Assessment/Plan: Sepsis secondary to community-acquired pneumonia. Admit to ICU.
